one_time_description: Prior period adjustment: Investment in Nuxalk Development Limited Partnership had been understated in prior year, restating Investment in GBEs, net assets, and surplus upward by $1,078,663
economic_notes: COVID-19 pandemic noted as impacting operations beginning March 2020. Nuxalk Development LP, Nuxalk Business Trust, and Nuxalk Motel (0820770 B.C. Ltd.) are government business enterprises. Social housing replacement reserve overfunded by $477,648 as at March 31, 2020.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED for two reasons: (1) Social Housing tangible capital assets amortized at CMHC-required rate (principal reduction basis) rather than useful life per PSAS; (2) GBEs prepared under ASPE rather than IFRS. Prior period adjustment of $1,078,663 restating 2019 comparative figures for understated investment in Nuxalk Development LP. Accumulated surplus on statement of financial position shows $61,260,374 but note 9 shows $31,005,158 - difference likely relates to GBE investments and restatement presentation. Three consolidated subsidiaries: Nuxalk Acwsalcmalslayc Academy of Learning Society, Nuxalk Nation Property Management, Nuxalk Nation Transition House Society. Three GBEs accounted for by modified equity method: Nuxalk Business Trust, Nuxalk Development LP, 0820770 B.C. Ltd. (Nuxalk Motel). COVID-19 pandemic noted. Auditor firm name not fully identified - signed as Chartered Professional Accountants, Kamloops BC. Total government transfers estimated as ISC + FNHA + Province of BC + DFO + FNESC + CMHC + Mid Coast First Nations Training Society + Coast Opportunity Funds.
